free_text: Study of photonuclear reactions in 165Ho nucleus at
the LINAC-200 accelerator.
Brno University of Technology, Brno,
Czech Republic.
Electron accelerator LINAC-200  of the
Joint institute for Nuclear Recearch (Dubna).
Natural holmium samples.
Induced gamma-activity was measured.
High purity germanium HPGe detector.
The relative yields (presented in Fig. 1) were
normalized to one electron incident on the lead
converter and measured for electrons with energies
of 60, 80, and 100 MeV. However, by interpolation,
the values can be found in the energy range from 50
to 110 MeV.
To determine the cross section of photonuclear
reactions based on the experimentally measured
reaction yield the method for calculating the
quasimonoenergetic spectrum from bremsstrahlung
spectrum was used.
This method permits determining reaction cross
sections via measuring the reaction yield at three
fixed values of the electron energy. The procedure
is equivalent to measuring the cross section in
question for quasimonochromatic photons.
Bremsstrahlung-photon spectra are obtained using
state-of-the-art simulation means involving the
GEANT4 code as applied to a specific experiment with
allowance for its geometry, target thicknesses,
electron-beam parameters, etc.
It is not described what yields from presented in
Fig. 1 were measured experimentally.
The energy scales in all parts of Fig. 1 looks
strange - two values equal to 60 MeV.
Description of FACILITY and detailes of experiment.
Description of the method of ANALYSIS.
Experimentally measured reaction yields.
No information on sources of uncertainties.
Data from the Table 1 of
Phys.Part.Nucl.Lett.19,347(2022).
Data  were  compiled at the MSU SINP CDFE
by V.V.Varlamov.
